STRATEGY // CREATIVE // DATA
01 / ABOUT

The Short Brief

Marketing and Communications professional with a Graduate Certificate in Strategic Marketing Communications and hands-on experience in campaign planning, social media strategy, content creation, and market research.

Skilled in translating consumer insights and performance data into actionable marketing strategies, with a strong foundation in copywriting, digital analytics, and event coordination. Proven ability to manage multiple projects and stakeholders in fast-paced environments while maintaining consistent brand messaging.

Outside of coursework, I've run media relations for a real nonprofit fundraiser, administered a live campus event, and built a second skill set in SQL, Python and Tableau — because the best marketing decisions are the ones you can actually measure.

05 / CASE STUDIES

The Binder

Six projects, six different marketing disciplines. Click any case to open the file.

  • PR & Media

    KW Legacy — Nonprofit Media Relations Campaign

    Full media kit & press outreach for a $1M+ scholarship fundraiser
     CASE NO. 01

    Role

    Media Relations Specialist

    What I did

    • Built a complete media kit for KW Legacy's inaugural Christmas Cookie Exchange — a family fundraiser event for their scholarship program.
    • Wrote an organizational fact sheet, media release, and a photo gallery spread of past events (Golf for Scholarships, Top Home Chefs).
    • Drafted individually targeted pitch letters to radio (570 News Kitchener), TV (CTV News Kitchener) and print (Waterloo Region Record), each tailored to that outlet's format.

    Outcome

    Positioned a first-year event inside KW Legacy's $1M+ raised / 96 scholarships track record to strengthen media credibility
  • Events

    Thrift & Thrive — Sustainable Fashion Event

    Sponsorship, planning & live execution — 93% over attendance goal
     CASE NO. 02

    Role

    Event Administrator, Evam Events (Conestoga College)

    What I did

    • Co-built the sponsorship proposal (Gold/Silver/Bronze tiers) that funded "Thrift and Thrive," a campus event promoting sustainable thrifting over fast fashion.
    • Helped plan the full event: venue layout, run of show, an Eco Pop-a-Cup game, an upcycled art studio, a guest keynote, and a screening — then administered it live on the day.

    Outcome

    58 attendees vs. a goal of 30 (93% over target) · funds raised donated to the CSI Food Bank
  • Strategy

    Mavericks Capstone — Fast Fashion Overconsumption

    A $237K "Wear It Again" awareness campaign, built from the ground up
     CASE NO. 03
    $237,220 CAMPAIGN BUDGET

    Role

    Strategic lead, capstone research group

    What I did

    • Diagnosed the environmental and behavioral roots of fast fashion overconsumption in Canada through market, competitor and target-audience research.
    • Designed the "Wear It Again" campaign: a documentary-led awareness strategy across Instagram and YouTube, with sustainability-influencer partnerships and paid search.
    • Set measurable goals: 2M consumers engaged in circular fashion by 2026, 25% reduction in carbon emissions, 30% reduction in textile waste, 80% audience reach within one year.

    Outcome

    KPI targets: 10% engagement rate · 100K+ views across platforms in 6 months
  • Creative

    NAV Advertising — Creative Campaign Concepts

    Ford Bronco relaunch campaign + Career Survival Challenge poster
     CASE NO. 04

    Role

    Founder & Creative, NAV Advertising (personal creative brand)

    What I did

    • Designed a full concept campaign for the Ford Bronco relaunch under my own creative studio brand — product positioning, target psychographics, competitor teardown (Jeep Wrangler, Land Rover Defender), media channel plan, and a "Time-Tested Toughness, Future-Ready Fun" concept ad pairing the 1966 and 2024 models.
    • Designed the poster and campaign concept for a Conestoga College "Career Survival Challenge" student event, in a bold Squid-Game-inspired visual language.

    Outcome

    Two fully art-directed concept campaigns, from research through final creative
  • Strategy

    Get Canada Cooking — Impossible Foods Market Entry

    Go-to-market plan for plant-based meat in the Canadian grocery aisle
     CASE NO. 05
    $7.5B → $15.8B MARKET

    Role

    Group strategist

    What I did

    • Researched barriers to adoption for plant-based meat (time-consuming prep, limited awareness, resistance to experimentation) to shape the entry strategy.
    • Built a value proposition map connecting customer jobs-to-be-done to Impossible Foods' product line (burger patties, ground meat, sausage, nuggets).
    • Recommended a three-part go-to-market plan: influencer/chef marketing, in-store sampling & education, and pre-seasoned product development.

    Outcome

    A full campaign framework spanning awareness, trial and product strategy
  • PR & Strategy

    Pinnacle Communications — PR Plan for The Scented Market

    Full PR audit and priority-publics strategy for an eco-lifestyle brand
     CASE NO. 06
    4 PUBLICS MAPPED

    Role

    PR strategist, client plan group

    What I did

    • Conducted a full PR audit of The Scented Market — internal performance, structure and impediments, plus external supporters, competitors and reputation — closing with a SWOT analysis.
    • Mapped the brand's full public landscape across customer, producer, enabler and limiter groups.
    • Selected and built targeted strategy for two priority publics: former trial customers and opinion-leader influencers.

    Outcome

    A prioritized, publics-first PR plan ready for client execution
06 / DATA-DRIVEN MARKETING

Behind the Numbers

Alongside the Strategic Marketing Communications certificate, I completed a Data Analytics Certificate through Correlation One / Amazon Career Choice — because campaign decisions land better with a dashboard behind them.

01 · SQL / TABLEAU

Payment Funnel Analysis

Analyzed 10,000 e-commerce sessions in SQL (CTEs, window functions) to diagnose a 70.95% checkout abandonment rate, then segmented the funnel by device, channel and product category.

$47K+ revenue opportunity surfaced
View Repo →
02 · PYTHON / EDA

Customer Behaviour EDA

Performed exploratory data analysis in Python (Pandas, Seaborn) on a 254-respondent shopping survey to uncover underserved segments and category preferences.

Tier 2 cities = 48% of underserved demand
View Repo →
03 · STATISTICAL ANALYSIS

Sports Car Price Analysis

Cleaned and analyzed a 1,007-vehicle dataset, ran Pearson correlation analysis, and caught a 10,000 HP data-entry error before it skewed the model — publishing findings via Tableau Public.

Horsepower → price, r = 0.80
View Repo →
